Down Syndrome Daily: Eat.
Move.
Lose.
Devereux, clinical dietitian Area Hospital, provides nutritional counseling patients with diabetes, heart disease other problems related obesity.
don't diet.
Instead, Devereux preaches long-term lifestyle change.
Nothing radical just healthy choices, smaller portions, routine physical activity.
"We're perfect people," says.
"But life made small choices.
Devereux looks slow, steady weight loss pounds week.
progress hasn't required extraordinary sacrifice.
Greg meets with Devereux every couple weeks.
During typical 15-minute appointment, talks with Greg about what he's been eating much been exercising.
Visiting Devereux late December, Greg reports that standard breakfast bowl instant oatmeal slice toast.
Grilled-cheese sandwiches, formerly favorite start day, history.
drinks water instead Gatorade.
Baked chicken replaced fried, eats lots salad.
Sweets rare.
likes pizza, slices limit.
Devereux asks about exercise.
Greg's mother, Frances Williams, reports that Greg progressed from three four trips down their long, rural driveway each day.
frequently rides exercise bike sister's nearby home.
does sit-ups.
seems working.
Devereux's target Greg this late-December visit pounds.
digital scale office reads triumphant prompting hugs high fives around.
"Not gain back," says.
meet that objective, Greg's short-term achievements need become permanent lifestyle changes.
anyone loses weight 2015, that's ideal outcome.
